text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real yet-another-react-lightbox repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: this library's root component figures out whether the page/container it is rendered into uses right-to-left (RTL) text direction, and uses that to decide things like which arrow key means 'next slide'. It detects the direction by reading the computed CSS `direction` of its own root element (via `getComputedStyle`). The problem is that this detection only ever runs once, at the moment the lightbox's root element is first attached to the DOM — the result is then cached in component state for the lifetime of that mount and never rechecked. So if whatever controls the computed direction changes later — for example an ancestor element's `dir` attribute is toggled from `ltr` to `rtl` at runtime, after the lightbox has already mounted — the lightbox does not notice. It keeps behaving as if the direction were still whatever it detected at mount time (e.g. arrow keys keep working in the old direction's sense) instead of picking up the new, current direction.

Environment notes: dependencies are already installed (Node, npm). You can scope the existing Vitest suite to whatever file you're iterating on with `npx vitest run <path-to-file>`. Fix the library source; do not just add workarounds in test files.

text: I’ll trace the root component’s direction detection and its existing tests, then update the source with the smallest lifecycle-safe fix and run the focused Vitest coverage plus relevant checks.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "pwd && rg --files -g '"'!*node_modules*'"' | head -80 && printf '\\n--- direction references ---\\n' && rg -n \"getComputedStyle|direction|rtl|RightToLeft|isRtl\" . -g '"'!node_modules'"' -g '"'!dist'"' | head -200"

text: The stale value is isolated in `LightboxRoot`: the callback ref sets state only when the DOM node is first assigned. I’m checking the project’s browser-support patterns and test setup now so the recheck mechanism fits the library rather than introducing a broad polling workaround.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "cat package.json && printf '\\n--- test setup ---\\n' && sed -n '1,220p' test/unit/setup.ts && printf '\\n--- tsconfig ---\\n' && cat tsconfig.json"

text: A `MutationObserver` is the appropriate trigger here: direction inheritance can change without any React prop/state update, so re-reading during render would still miss it. I’ll observe attribute changes in the owning document, re-evaluate the mounted root, and add a regression test that toggles an ancestor’s `dir` after mount.

text: The focused run confirms the observer path is active, while the new assertion was placed inside an already-RTL fixture, so its initial slide expectation was invalid. I’m moving the regression case into a genuinely LTR ancestor fixture and then I’ll run lint/type checks for the touched code.

text:
Fixed RTL direction staleness in [LightboxRoot.tsx](/home/user/workspace/src/components/LightboxRoot.tsx:21) by rechecking computed direction through a `MutationObserver`.

Added a regression test for runtime ancestor `dir` changes in [RTL.spec.ts](/home/user/workspace/test/unit/RTL.spec.ts:143).

Verified:

- 75 unit tests passing
- ESLint passing
- TypeScript check remains blocked by existing `test/types` package-resolution errors unrelated to this change
